rubrics:
Вечірня: катизма чергова. На «Господи, взиваю» 6 стихир: 3 Октоїха, 3 мч., Сл., І нині: хрестобогород. Прокімен дня. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар мч., Сл., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря мч. і за днем тижня. Відпуст середній з помин. мч.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар мч. (2), Сл., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря мч. Катизми черг., сідальні Октоїха. Пс. 50. Канон Октоїха і мч. На 9-ій пісні – «Чеснішу від херувимів». По 9-ій – світильний Октоїха, Сл.: світильний мч., І нині: його богород. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар мч., Сл., І нині: богород. за гласом тропаря мч. і за днем тижня. Відпуст середній з помин. мч.
Часи: на 1-му – дня; на 6-му – храму; на 3-му та 9-му – мч.
Літургія: вся Служба – дня. Ап. – Кол. 253 зач.; 2, 1-7. Єв. – Лк. 42 зач.; 9, 12-17.

troparion_en.text: Longinus, you beheld the King of Glory / who was nailed to the Cross, yet shone on those in darkness. / You were enlightened by His rays and became a martyr / and save those who cry: / Glory to Him who gave you strength! / Glory to Him who granted you a crown! / Glory to Him who through you grants healing to all!

kontakion_en.text: The Church rejoices in gladness / on the day of the commemoration of the ever-memorable athlete Longinus! / She cries out: “You are my strength and support, O Christ!”

matched_oca_saint: Martyr Longinus the Centurion, who stood at the Cross of the Lord

oca_saints.name: Martyr Longinus the Centurion, who stood at the Cross of the Lord

troparion.text: Longinus, you beheld the King of Glory / who was nailed to the Cross, yet shone on those in darkness. / You were enlightened by His rays and became a martyr / and save those who cry: / Glory to Him who gave you strength! / Glory to Him who granted you a crown! / Glory to Him who through you grants healing to all!

kontakion.text: The Church rejoices in gladness / on the day of the commemoration of the ever-memorable athlete Longinus! / She cries out: “You are my strength and support, O Christ!”

oca_saints.name: Venerable Longinus the Gatekeeper of the Kiev Caves

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Longinus, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Longinus, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

Your holy martyr Longinus, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Longinus;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

troparion.text: In you the image was preserved with exactness, O Mother,  / for taking up your cross, you followed after Christ.  / By so doing, you taught us to disregard the flesh, for it passes away,  / but to care instead for the soul, since it is immortal.  / Therefore, most venerable Mother Eupraxia your spirit rejoices with the Angels.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “Having received the grace...“)
Because of your love of God you forsook a bed for rest, O Mother Eupraxia, /  you illumined your soul with fasting, thoroughly vanquishing your enemies; /  but by your intercessions destroy the evil designs of our adversaries.

troparion.text: Hearing the voice of Thine Apostle Paul say: “We are fools for Christ;”  /  Thy handmaiden Mother Domna, O Christ God, was a fool on earth for Thy sake;  /  therefore, honoring her memory,  /  we entreat Thee: “O Lord, save our souls!”
